message:
feat(wasm): implement browser host I/O wiring for storage and streams

Wire the previously-stubbed browser I/O modules to actual Web API backends
via wasm-bindgen, replacing the scaffold implementations with working
host-backed code:

- Cargo.toml: Add wasm32 target dependencies — js-sys, wasm-bindgen,
  wasm-bindgen-futures, and web-sys with features for Window, Storage,
  ReadableStream, ReadableStreamDefaultReader, WritableStream, and
  WritableStreamDefaultWriter.

- src/io/browser_storage.rs: Implement host-backed localStorage/sessionStorage
  operations via web_sys::Storage. Add BrowserStorageError::HostBackend
  variant for surfacing JS-side errors. Native builds retain the in-memory
  BTreeMap stub. Wasm32 builds use base64-encoded keys for binary-safe
  storage and properly propagate quota/security errors from the host.

- src/io/browser_stream.rs: Implement ReadableStream/WritableStream bridging
  via web_sys types. Add BrowserReadStream wrapping ReadableStreamDefaultReader
  with async chunk-by-chunk reading, and BrowserWriteStream wrapping
  WritableStreamDefaultWriter with proper backpressure handling. Native
  builds retain the existing stub implementations.

- src/io/cap.rs: Add new tests for FetchAuthority — method denial and
  header budget enforcement — improving coverage of the capability-based
  I/O permission system.

- src/io/mod.rs: Re-export the new browser I/O types.

- src/net/websocket/close.rs: Add WebSocket close code constants for
  browser-initiated closures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -201,6 +201,17 @@ io-uring = { version = "0.7.11", optional = true }
 # wasm32 browser builds require JS-backed entropy wiring for getrandom.
 [target.'cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")'.dependencies]
 getrandom = { version = "0.4", features = ["wasm_js"] }
+js-sys = "0.3"
+wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
+wasm-bindgen-futures = "0.4"
+web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = [
+    "Window",
+    "Storage",
+    "ReadableStream",
+    "ReadableStreamDefaultReader",
+    "WritableStream",
+    "WritableStreamDefaultWriter",
+] }
 
 # Native-only dependencies: platform I/O, sockets, signals, and crypto.
 # These are excluded from wasm32 browser builds (umelq.3.3).
